sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports left shoulder pain for approximately three weeks, with no specific inciting event. He has been renovating his basement and reports significant activity. He denies any trauma. He has taken Tylenol PRN, reporting a pain level of 7 without medication and 3 with Tylenol. He states that he cannot lay on his left side due to pain.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals limited range of motion in the left shoulder, with tenderness to palpation of the greater tuberosity. Neurovascular exam is intact; cap refill brisk. X-ray shows no fracture or bony abnormality.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy. An MRI is ordered to rule out other pathology.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering an MRI of the left shoulder. I am also referring the patient for physical therapy, starting with a 6-8 week duration. Continue taking Tylenol PRN as needed. Follow up in approximately two weeks to assess progress.
